Title: Three Mutations in Zea mays Affecting Zein Accumulation: A Comparison of Zein Polypeptides, In Vitro Synthesis and Processing, mRNA Levels, and Genomic Organization

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major endosperm storage protein; low in lysine and tryptophan, so reduction improves grain nutritional quality
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm protein bodies in rough endoplasmic reticulum-derived vesicles
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Causes major reduction of zein accumulation, especially strong suppression of most 22.5-kDa zein polypeptides, with minor effects on some 19-kDa components
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 2D IEF:SDS-PAGE of zein polypeptides; in vitro translation/processing with RER membranes; RNA blot hybridization with zein cDNA probes; Southern blot analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional effect studied in developing endosperm 18-20 days after pollination
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Regulatory locus/mutation; specific protein identity not defined in paper
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing self-pollinated kernels harvested 18-20 days after pollination
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. o2 strongly suppresses accumulation of the 22.5-kDa zein class in multiple maize backgrounds. 2. RER membranes from o2 are competent for prezein processing, so the defect is not in membrane processing. 3. A specific subgroup of 22.5-kDa zein mRNAs detected by probe B14 is greatly reduced in o2. 4. Southern analysis argues against a large deletion of tandem-linked zein genes as the cause.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper states o2 had long been proposed as a regulatory locus for zein synthesis, but does not identify the gene product. Reduction of zein is associated with improved nutritional grain quality through reciprocal increase in non-zein storage proteins.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major endosperm storage protein; low in lysine and tryptophan, so reduction improves grain nutritional quality
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm protein bodies in rough endoplasmic reticulum-derived vesicles
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces total zein content with subtler qualitative changes than o2; some spots absent from both 19- and 22.5-kDa zein classes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 2D IEF:SDS-PAGE of zein polypeptides; in vitro translation/processing with RER membranes; RNA blot hybridization with zein cDNA probes
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional effect studied in developing endosperm 18-20 days after pollination
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Mutation/locus; specific protein identity not defined in paper
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing self-pollinated kernels harvested 18-20 days after pollination
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. o7 lowers total zein accumulation. 2. 2D gels show absence of some minor zein spots in both major size classes. 3. Mutant RER membranes still correctly process wild-type zein mRNAs, arguing against a processing defect. 4. The effect appears more general on zein synthesis than the class-selective o2 phenotype.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper concludes o7 does not show the class-specific suppression seen in o2 and likely causes a general inhibition affecting multiple zein classes.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major endosperm storage protein; low in lysine and tryptophan, so reduction improves grain nutritional quality
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm protein bodies in rough endoplasmic reticulum-derived vesicles
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces total zein content; 2D gels suggest missing spots mainly from the lower molecular weight zein class and in vitro synthesis shows mostly 19-kDa zeins plus a minor ~25-kDa band
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 2D IEF:SDS-PAGE of zein polypeptides; in vitro translation/processing with RER membranes; RNA blot hybridization with zein cDNA probes
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional effect studied in developing endosperm 18-20 days after pollination
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Mutation/locus; specific protein identity not defined in paper
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing self-pollinated kernels harvested 18-20 days after pollination
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. fl2 decreases total zein accumulation. 2. 2D gels indicate loss of some lower molecular weight zein components. 3. Mutant membranes process prezeins efficiently, so reduced zein content is not explained by defective RER processing. 4. The mutation appears to cause a general inhibition distinct from o2.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats fl2 as a core mutant affecting zein accumulation, but does not identify the underlying molecular gene product.
